response.justification: Bogdan is a versatile, craftsmanship-oriented engineer with genuine high-throughput distributed systems experience (millions of HTTP requests per second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic) and current hands-on work on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling, which fits the AI-native environment well. However, Python — a must-have — appears nowhere on the resume (his systems depth is in DLang and Node/TypeScript), and evidence of staff-level technical strategy, cross-functional stakeholder leadership, and operational on-call ownership at scale is thin. Strong TypeScript exceeds the working-level bar, but the Python gap and unproven strategy scope keep this from a clear yes.
strengths: Real high-throughput, low-latency distributed systems experience (millions of requests/sec, async I/O, caching)
Current hands-on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP/agentic-workflow tooling experience at a Staff level
Strong TypeScript/Node full-stack depth plus broad polyglot range and testing/TDD discipline
concerns: No Python listed anywhere — a must-have skill for this role
Little demonstrated experience directing technical strategy or coordinating engineering/product/leadership stakeholders at scale
Deepest systems work is in the niche DLang ecosystem and dates to 2020; recent roles skew product/full-stack rather than large-scale reliability ownership
